title: Multi-task Loss
concept: Multi-task Loss combines classification loss and bounding box regression loss into a single function, enabling joint end-to-end training.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What type of pooling is used in the RoI Pooling layer?
exercises.explain: RoI Pooling divides regions into sub-sections and applies max pooling.

cards.title: Joint Learning
cards.body: If you are studying to be a pilot, you don't attend one school for takeoff, graduate, then go to a second school for landing. You practice both skills in the same flight simulator, and you are graded on both together. Multi-task loss allows the network to practice both tasks together.

cards.title: Multi-task Loss
cards.body: Multi-task Loss combines classification loss (like log loss over classes) and bounding box regression loss (smooth L1 loss over coordinates) into a single unified loss function: $L = L_{cls} + \lambda L_{loc}$, where $\lambda$ scales the balance.

cards.title: The Combined Grade
cards.body: Think of a student's final grade in a science class. It's a combination of their lab report score (accuracy of classification) and their math calculation score (accuracy of location coordinates). The teacher averages them to give one grade.

cards.title: Smooth L1 Loss
cards.body: Fast R-CNN uses smooth L1 loss for box regression because it is less sensitive to outliers than L2 loss. If the offset error is small, it acts like L2; if the error is large, it acts like L1, preventing exploding gradients.

cards.title: No separate training stages
cards.body: It's a myth that classification and localization models must be trained in separate phases. By combining them in a single loss function, gradients flow back through the shared layers, improving both tasks simultaneously.

cards.title: Split Output Heads
cards.body: Visualizing how two output heads share a single backpropagation pass:

left.text:
Log loss / Softmax cross-entropy.
Graded on category name.

right.text:
Smooth L1 loss.
Graded on coordinate offsets.

exercises.prompt: What does Fast R-CNN use to train both classification and localization together?
exercises.explain: Fast R-CNN combines classification loss and regression loss into a single Multi-task Loss function for joint training.

options.text: A Multi-task Loss function that combines classification and regression costs
Two separate backpropagation passes on different GPUs

exercises.prompt: True or False: Smooth L1 loss is used for the classification head in Fast R-CNN.
exercises.explain: False. Smooth L1 loss is used for the localization (bounding box regression) head. Classification uses standard log loss (cross-entropy).
Smooth L1 loss prevents extreme gradient updates from outlier coordinate predictions during training.
exercises.sentence: Fast R-CNN uses ___ loss for bounding box regression because it is robust to coordinate outliers.

exercises.prompt: Which formula represents a multi-task loss function for detection?
exercises.explain: Combining classification and localization losses with a balancing weight (lambda) is the multi-task loss formula.

exercises.prompt: You are training a Fast R-CNN model. If the localization loss has a balancing weight (lambda) set to 0, what will happen to the network during backpropagation?

options.text: The network will only optimize for classification, and the bounding box coordinate outputs will not improve.
The network will train twice as fast with no accuracy drop.
The network will stop classifying background pixels.
The weights of the backbone will freeze.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Setting lambda to 0 completely zeroes out the localization loss gradient, meaning the model will only learn to classify and ignore bounding box accuracy. Next: let's look at the remaining speed bottleneck in Fast R-CNN!
